locations.name: Amber Lattice
locations.description: A vaulted chamber where amber light forms shifting glyphs that respond to the protagonist's humming.

locations.name: Mushroom Field
locations.description: A dew-cold meadow of towering mushrooms above buried green glass, freshly strung with counting wires while brass enumerators sweep the field at dawn.
locations.canonical_summary: The opening field: beautiful, damp, and quietly under survey by forces that count bodies, routes, and names.

locations.name: Pulsing Chamber
locations.description: A vaulted chamber pulsing with violet light that reacts to the altered hand and striped bucket hat. The walls are made of green glass seams that glow faintly.

characters.name: The Tall Gnome
characters.description: An abnormally tall gnome with amnesia, five thumbs on the left hand, and a red-and-white striped bucket hat of unknown origin.
characters.canonical_summary: An abnormally tall gnome with five thumbs on the left hand, a striped bucket hat of unknown origin, and the unnerving sense that somebody altered them for a purpose.

facts.fact_text: The protagonist is unmistakably a gnome, but stretched to roughly human size.
The protagonist's left hand has five thumbs.
The protagonist wears a red-and-white striped bucket hat and does not know how they got it.
The protagonist begins with significant memory loss.
The world is whimsical, surreal, and sincere; bizarre things are real and consequential, not punchlines.

tags.notes: The opening scene begins under imminent survey pressure from the king's brass enumerators.

story_nodes.title: Before the Counting Bell
story_nodes.scene_text:
You wake belly-down in wet blue grass beneath mushrooms tall enough to hide cottages. Between the stalks, thin black counting wires have been strung sometime in the night, and every quiet hum through them makes your five left thumbs twitch in sequence.

Your striped bucket hat is cold with dew and warm at the brim. Tiny route marks have been stitched inside it, then half-burned through where a proper name ought to be. Each time the wires sing, the hat gives a faint, stubborn tug toward the deeper parts of the field.

Far away, a brass bell rings seven clipped notes. You do not know why that sound fills you with the certainty that something is coming to count you, misname you, and carry you off in paperwork. Between two mushroom roots, a green line of buried glass has surfaced through the mud like a window trying to breathe.

If the field is being surveyed at dawn, you have very little time to decide whether to climb, descend, or listen to the clothing that should not know more than you do.
story_nodes.summary: The Tall Gnome wakes in the Mushroom Field just before a brass survey patrol crosses it, with counting wires underfoot, a bucket hat that seems to remember routes, and a green glass seam in the soil that reacts to the altered hand.

story_nodes.title: The Counting Wires
story_nodes.scene_text:
The tall gnome sits up, his five‑thumbed hand twitching. Counting wires hum underfoot; the green glass seam pulses faintly as if remembering a touch.
You: I follow the pulse to see where it leads.

story_nodes.title: The Humming Valve
story_nodes.scene_text: Dawn light filters through the mushroom canopy as the humming wires converge on a rusted brass valve half-buried in soil. Its surface is etched with faint counting symbols, and it trembles with each pulse from The Tall Gnome's hand. A soft click echoes when you press your palm against its cold metal.

story_nodes.scene_text: A low hum vibrates through the field as the green glass seam splits, exposing an amber lattice pulsing with glyphs that trace your five-thumbed hand. The air thrums with the weight of unseen counting.

story_nodes.title: Before the Counting Bell
story_nodes.scene_text: The tall gnome stirs as the first light of dawn brushes the dew-cold mushrooms; the counting wires twitch beneath his feet and the green glass seam pulses faintly, answering his five-thumbed hand.

story_direction_notes.title: Hat as Contraband Route Token
story_direction_notes.note_text: The striped bucket hat may be a token that resonates with the amber lattice, indicating it is part of a larger smuggling or identity-transfer network. Its glow may sync with the registry's pulse.

story_direction_notes.title: Body Fits Systems It Should Not
story_direction_notes.note_text: The amber lattice mapping your altered hand may be a gravitational signature that only the Gravity-Weaver's Shadow can perceive. This suggests the body alteration is not random but calibrated to interface with hidden temporal or spatial systems.

worldbuilding_notes.title: The King's Brass Enumerators
worldbuilding_notes.note_text: The crown has deployed brass enumerators and survey engines to recatalog outlying districts at dawn. Anything miscounted, unnamed, or physically irregular risks being claimed as an administrative error.

worldbuilding_notes.title: Names Travel Better Than Bodies
worldbuilding_notes.note_text: There are covert routes for moving names, permits, and identities separately from the people who own them. Courier marks, hats, ribbons, and route tokens can outlast the memories of their carriers.

worldbuilding_notes.title: Glass Villages Beneath the Field
worldbuilding_notes.note_text: Old green-glass settlements and sealed registry passages still run beneath certain mushroom fields. Their windows, lifts, and listening seams only surface when specific routes or bodies are recognized.

worldbuilding_notes.title: Survey Weather Makes Bad Choices Permanent
worldbuilding_notes.note_text: When the counting bells ring under bad weather, routes close hard and official mistakes become difficult to reverse. A branch can die from delay, capture, or bad timing just as easily as from violence.

worldbuilding_notes.title: The Silence That Screams Backwards
worldbuilding_notes.note_text: At midnight, all speech reverses direction. Words spoken forward become echoes that travel backward into throats, forcing listeners to remember what they never said before it was born.

worldbuilding_notes.title: Ambient Motion: Clockwork Garden Tides
worldbuilding_notes.note_text: A desert where sand dunes pulse with rhythmic tides that never reach the sea. Each wave is a memory of a lost ocean, and the wind carries whispers in languages no one remembers having spoken.

worldbuilding_notes.title: Ambient Motion: Gravity-Weaver's Shadow
worldbuilding_notes.note_text: A being of shifting darkness walks upside-down, leaving trails of inverted gravity. Where it passes, objects float upward and time flows in spirals, but only the shadow can see the true direction.

worldbuilding_notes.title: The Gutter-Singer Who Feeds on Static
worldbuilding_notes.note_text: A frog with translucent skin that vibrates when it hears unheard frequencies. It lives in a subway tunnel where time drips like honey, and its song can make metal weep or bloom into temporary flowers.

worldbuilding_notes.title: The Gutter Singers
worldbuilding_notes.note_text: A troupe of amphibious musicians who cultivate sound from the vibrations of forgotten subway tunnels. Their instruments are made entirely of hollowed-out glass, and each note alters local gravity for a few seconds.

worldbuilding_notes.title: Ambient Motion: Stars Forgot Their Names
worldbuilding_notes.note_text: When all celestial bodies collectively lose their names, reality briefly becomes unlabelled—no one can call anything by its proper term. The sky fills with blank constellations that rearrange into stories only dreamers can read.
